# services.meilisearch

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.meilisearch`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.meilisearch.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Meilisearch - a RESTful search API. |
| `services.meilisearch.listenAddress` | `string` | The IP address that Meilisearch will listen on. It can also be a hostname like "localhost". If it resolves to an IPv4 and IPv6 address, Meilisearch will listen on both. |
| `services.meilisearch.listenPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port that Meilisearch will listen on. |
| `services.meilisearch.masterKeyFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to file which contains the master key. By doing so, all routes will be protected and will require a key to be accessed. If no master key is provided, all routes can be accessed without requiring any key. You can generate a master key by running `openssl rand -base64 36`. Alternatively, you can start Meilisearch without a master key and use the pre-generated key from the service's logs that can be obtained by `journalctl -u meilisearch \| grep -- --master-key`. |
| `services.meilisearch.package` | `package` | The meilisearch package to use. Use this if you require specific features to be enabled. The default package has no features. |
| `services.meilisearch.settings` | `open submodule of (TOML value)` | Configuration settings for Meilisearch. Look at the documentation for available options: https://github.com/meilisearch/meilisearch/blob/main/config.toml https://www.meilisearch.com/docs/learn/self_hosted/configure_meilisearch_at_launch#all-instance-options |
